title: erophila verna subsp. stenocarpa
intro: Erophila verna subsp. stenocarpa, also known as early spring whitlowgrass, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. It is a perennial herb with medicinal properties, particularly in its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is said to have health benefits, including reducing fever and soothing respiratory issues due to its expectorant and antitussive properties. Early spring whitlowgrass can be used in herbal preparations, such as infusions and decoctions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
